Theatro, a maker of AI and voice-powered communication and digital workflow software for frontline workers for $174 million

AI-powered analytics can highlight a person at a facility out of hours (unusual activity), locate a missing child at a theme park (appearance search), automate video verification workflows for building access

Assist, our mission-critical AI, operationalizes intelligence across the command center... enables 911 transcription, live translation and narrative development to accelerate response and enhance reporting accuracy
SVX body-worn assistant exemplifies this strategy by converging secure voice, video and AI into a single device to offer a highly differentiated solution
AI Act places significant restrictions on the use of AI for real-time biometric identification by law enforcement, and implements significant compliance requirements on the development and use of AI
if we use AI that is based on data, algorithms, or other inputs that are flawed or insufficient... our business, financial condition, or results of operations may be adversely affected
we work to responsibly meet our customers needs... including through AI governance programs and internal technology oversight committees, we may still suffer reputational damage

capex_mentions.quote: Blue Eye, a provider of AI-powered enterprise remote video monitoring ('RVM') services for $79 million
capex_mentions.context: Acquisition of AI-powered RVM provider, November 2025

capex_mentions.quote: Theatro, a maker of AI and voice-powered communication and digital workflow software for frontline workers for $174 million
capex_mentions.context: Acquisition of AI voice/workflow software company, March 2025

capex_mentions.quote: Silvus Technologies Holdings Inc. ... $4.4 billion and share-based compensation of $20 million
capex_mentions.context: Acquisition of MANET technology company (not purely AI-labeled but included in AI-adjacent MCN portfolio), August 2025

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: Disclosed spend is primarily via acquisitions of AI-capable companies rather than direct AI capex or infrastructure build-out; no dedicated AI R&D budget or GPU/data-center spend is quantified. Score reflects quantified AI-related acquisition spend as the closest proxy.

ai_narrative_concreteness: Multiple named use cases described as deployed with operational detail, but no quantified business results (e.g., % improvement, volume handled) are present in the excerpts; score capped at 4.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and contains detailed, company-specific AI risk scenarios (regulatory, model error, governance) with named mechanisms; governance programs (AI governance programs, internal technology oversight committees) are named but not quantified, limiting score to 4 rather than 5.

Silvus acquisition ($4.4B) is primarily a MANET/communications technology deal, not purely AI; included in capex_mentions for completeness given its inclusion in AI-powered MCN portfolio, but confidence in AI attribution is lower.
